---
title: "LoRa RadioRocket"
description: "Amateur radio telemetry system for mid and high power model rockets, combining APRS tracking and LoRa data transmission with a Raspberry Pi ground station."
date: "2025-06-01"
categories: [IoT, telemetry, LoRa, APRS, hardware, amateur radio]
image: ../images/lora-radiorocket.jpg
---

## Overview

RadioRocketV2 is an open-source amateur radio project integrating modern digital 
radio systems into mid and high power model rocketry. This fork of the 
[N3VEM RadioRocketV2](https://github.com/N3VEM/RadioRocketV2) project adds 
updated documentation to make the build more accessible.

## System Components

### APRS Tracking
A self-contained APRS beacon sled provides position tracking and mapping during 
flight. Packets are received by any I-Gate within range and viewable on 
[aprs.fi](https://aprs.fi). Can be built standalone without the rest of the system.

### LoRa Telemetry
The rocket transmits telemetry over 70cm LoRa, including sensor data, relay 
messages, and command responses. LoRa's long range and low power make it ideal 
for tracking rockets downrange.

### Ground Station
A portable ground station built around a single-board computer and LoRa Feather 
module receives telemetry and APRS data, serving it via Node-RED and a networked 
sound modem to any browser on the local network.

## Hardware

- Adafruit Feather with LoRa Radio (rocket-side)
- SDRplay SDR dongle (APRS reception)
- Raspberry Pi ground station
- Custom wiring and project enclosure

## Status

✅ *Working prototype complete — documentation in progress.*

## Links

- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/velezf/RadioRocketV2-documentation)
